channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Trump Criticizes German Chancellor Merz Over Iran Policy
item.description: President Trump told Chancellor Friedrich Merz to focus on ending the Russia-Ukraine war and spend "less time on interfering" with efforts to tackle the Iran nuclear threat.

item.title: Khamenei Vows to Protect Irans Nuclear and Missile Capabilities
item.description: Irans Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei declared that the Islamic Republic will defend its nuclear and missile capabilities as a national asset, signaling a hardline stance amid ongoing tensions with the US.

item.title: US-China Trade Chiefs Hold Candid Call Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
item.description: Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng had "candid, in-depth and constructive exchanges" with US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Trade Representative Jamieson Greer, signaling preparations for an expected leaders summit.

item.title: EU and Mercosur Implement Free Trade Agreement to Counter US Tariffs
item.description: The European Union and South American bloc Mercosur will implement their contentious free trade agreement on Friday, aiming to benefit exporters and offset the impact of US tariffs.

item.title: Inflation Hits 3 Percent in Europe as Iran War Spreads Oil Price Shock
item.description: Soaring oil prices from the Iran war pushed inflation higher to 3% in Europe in April, while growth continued to underperform.

item.title: Ukraine Parliament approves expansion of military cemeteries across regions
item.description: New law passed by Ukraine's parliament enables the creation of official military cemeteries in multiple regions, building on the national military cemetery opened near Kyiv earlier this year to honor fallen soldiers.

item.title: Multinational Asia operation arrests over 400 for online child exploitation
item.description: Police from Singapore, Malaysia, Thailand, Brunei, Hong Kong, Japan and South Korea arrested 326 and investigated 119 others in a month-long operation targeting online child sexual exploitation across the region.

item.title: Gulf states test Hormuz bypass infrastructure as strait remains blocked
item.description: With the Strait of Hormuz largely shut for two months due to the Iran war, alternative pipelines and routes are being stress-tested to their limits, revealing both capabilities and vulnerabilities in Gulf energy export infrastructure.

item.title: US Military Commanders to Brief Trump on New Iran Options
item.description: Top US military leaders including CENTCOM head Admiral Brad Cooper will brief President Trump on potential military action against Iran as the standoff over the Strait of Hormuz continues.

item.title: Oil Prices Hit Four-Year High Near $126 Before Retreating
item.description: Global oil prices surged to over $126 per barrel on concerns about the US-Iran conflict disrupting Middle East supply before retreating, as markets weigh the risk of protracted disruption.

item.title: China Conducts Combat Patrols Near Scarborough Shoal
item.description: China held combat readiness patrols near Scarborough Shoal in the disputed South China Sea as the Philippines and US staged joint drills in the region.

item.title: Ukraine to Seek Clarity From US on Russian Ceasefire Proposal
item.description: President Zelenskiy said Ukraine will seek clarification from the Trump team on details of a Russian proposal for a brief ceasefire next week.

item.title: IMF Warns Asia on Policy Balance Amid Iran War Disruptions
item.description: The IMF Director for Asia Pacific warned Asian countries to keep policy ready for future shocks even as they tackle the energy crisis from the Iran war.

item.title: ECB Holds Rates Steady, Debates Hike as Iran War Clouds Outlook
item.description: The European Central Bank left interest rates unchanged after debating a possible hike, as policymakers assess the economic fallout from the Iran war and surging energy prices.

item.title: Key Inflation Gauge Jumps to 3.5%, Highest in Three Years
item.description: The Fed's preferred inflation measure surged as Iran war-driven gas prices squeezed budgets. Core inflation hit 3.2%, keeping rate cuts on hold.

item.title: Paraguay President to Visit Taiwan as China Ramps Up Pressure
item.description: President Santiago Peña will make a state visit in May to reinforce diplomatic ties. Paraguay remains Taiwan's last South American ally as Beijing intensifies efforts to switch recognition.

item.title: Australia Seals Jet Fuel Cooperation Deal with China Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong announced China has agreed to cooperate on jet fuel exports, potentially loosening restrictions imposed during supply disruptions from the Iran war. The deal came during Wong's diplomatic tour of Northeast Asia.

item.title: Hezbollah Deploys Fiber-Optic Drones Adopted from Ukraine War
item.description: Hezbollah has introduced small drones controlled by fiber-optic cables that evade electronic detection, a technology first pioneered in Ukraine. The devices represent a new threat to Israeli defenses in the ongoing conflict.

item.title: Germany Overtakes UK as Europe's Largest Defense Spender
item.description: Germany has become Europe's top defense investor with 153 major projects underway, surpassing the UK. The Bundeswehr aims to become the "strongest conventional armed force in Europe" amid growing security concerns.

item.title: China replaces agriculture minister in second top-level shake-up in two years
item.description: Zhang Zhu appointed as agriculture minister following Communist Party leadership changes, marking the second major personnel shift at the ministry in two years amid ongoing agricultural policy reforms.

item.title: White House silent as China ramps up trade leverage before Trump-Xi summit
item.description: Beijing introduced new trade rules that alarmed US businesses. Analysts say the measures could undercut American efforts to reduce supply chain dependence on China ahead of the planned summit.

item.title: Kim Jong Un Praises North Korean Soldier Suicides in Ukraine War
item.description: At a ceremony unveiling a bronze sculpture of North Korean and Russian soldiers, Kim Jong Un explicitly praised troops who chose death over capture in Ukraine, the most direct acknowledgment yet of Pyongyang battlefield doctrine.

item.title: US Seeks International Coalition to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The State Department is pressing for an international coalition to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, according to a cable seen by Reuters, as oil prices surge on fears of prolonged supply disruption.

item.title: Russian Shadow Airlines Use Algeria as Base for Secretive Military Missions
item.description: Algeria has become a key hub for Russian military logistics as shadow airlines conduct covert missions, raising concerns about Moscow's expanding influence in North Africa.

item.title: Countries Pay Iran Transit Fees to Pass Through Strait of Hormuz Despite US Sanctions
item.description: China, India, Japan and other nations continue paying Iran transit fees to ship oil through the Strait of Hormuz, circumventing US sanctions and providing Tehran with revenue.
